prevalence.value: common seasonal illness; highest in children 6 months to 3 years

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis; severity scoring; imaging rarely required

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with steroids and guideline-based emergency care

regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dexamethasone for most; nebulized epinephrine for moderate-severe cases; observation protocols
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust emergency pathways and high access to effective treatments
